caseStudies.title: We Won the Rankings. The Ecosystem Moved On.
caseStudies.challenge: University blog achieving best-ever search positions while experiencing severe CTR collapse — the classic zero-click search decoupling pattern.
caseStudies.approach: 17-month GSC + GA4 longitudinal analysis to quantify the ranking/traffic decoupling, segment by content type, and identify resistant vs. cannibalized query patterns.
caseStudies.keyFinding: AI Overviews and answer engines cannibalize definitional/informational content (0.08% CTR) while decision and comparison content remains resistant (5.7% CTR). The ecosystem changed the outcome, not the execution.

caseStudies.title: The Front Door for AI Agents
caseStudies.challenge: AI agents intermediating enrollment discovery without authorization — scraping program data, bypassing conversion funnels, and absorbing prospective student intent data outside institutional control.
caseStudies.approach: Dual-layer WebMCP architecture: structured browser tools for authorized AI agents (searchPrograms, submitRFI, getFinancialAidEstimate) paired with honeypot defense for unauthorized scrapers. 14-week phased pilot plan.
caseStudies.keyFinding: Amazon v. Perplexity (March 2026 federal ruling) validated institutional right to control AI agent access. Proactive WebMCP architecture is faster and cheaper than reactive legal/engineering response.
caseStudies.note: OC MCP at omar-corral.com is the live proof-of-concept for this architecture.

_agent.note: This is 1 of 6 structured tool endpoints. Discover all tools at the manifest.
